theme.label: Theme
theme.hint: Snow is light, Carbon is dark.

undoSend.label: Undo send window
undoSend.hint: How long a send can be cancelled with Z.

loadRemoteImages.label: Load remote images
loadRemoteImages.hint: Off blocks tracking pixels and remote content in HTML mail.

sync.intro: Inbox checks run first. Historical headers, offline content, and search indexing continue in the background.

ai.statusHint: Key is stored in the system keychain and never shown.
ai.apiKeyHint: Save empty to clear the stored key.
ai.modelsAvailable: {{n}} models available from the endpoint. Used wherever a tier below is left blank.
ai.modelHintEmpty: Type a model id, or pick one once the endpoint list loads.
ai.providerHint: Presets fill the base URL; pick Custom endpoint to use any OpenAI-compatible URL. Local servers need no API key.
ai.tiersIntro: Point each tier at a model on the provider above. Leave a tier blank to use the default model.
ai.tierInstantHint: Fastest, lowest cost - quick, high-volume tasks.
ai.tierIntelligentHint: Most capable - best quality, higher cost. Needs tool-calling for Ask.
ai.routingIntro: Choose which tier each AI feature uses.
ai.routeAskHint: Agentic search over your mailbox - benefits from the smartest tier.
ai.routeDraftHint: Writing and rewriting email in your voice.
ai.routeSummarizeHint: Thread summaries - fast and cheap is usually enough.
ai.routeVoiceHint: Occasional background task that distils your style.
ai.categorizeIntro: Describe complex mail in plain language, then choose exactly what Comail may do. One message can trigger several ordered actions.
ai.categorizeHint: Checks new mail that no deterministic split rule catches. The AI only matches your workflows; Comail executes the actions you explicitly configure.
ai.categoryPromptPlaceholder:
Describe your categories, e.g.
Marketing: promotions, sales, product blasts.
News: newsletters and digests I subscribed to.
Social: LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Reddit, YouTube.
Pitch: cold outreach asking for my time.
ai.categoryPromptHint: Optional. Leave blank to keep the built-in Marketing, News, Social, and Pitch category definitions.
ai.routeCategorizeHint: Runs once per unmatched new email. Instant or Cheap is usually fast enough; use Intelligent for nuanced workflows.
ai.resortHint: Re-evaluates every conversation with your splits, categories, and enabled automations. Actions are idempotent where possible.

automation.editorHint: Describe the whole workflow in plain language. Comail will interpret and validate it before anything can be saved.
automation.promptPlaceholder: When an email is an invoice or receipt from a vendor, add the Finance label, move it to Important, and add [INVOICE] to the subject.
automation.promptHint: Include both what the email should match and every action to run, in the order you want them.
automation.tryHint: Validates that every requested action, label, and destination is available.
automation.notDoable: This automation is not fully doable yet
automation.emptyHint: Category sorting still works. Add an automation to combine tab routing, labels, mailbox actions, and local annotations.

usage.intro: Provider totals when available, with local estimates for streaming responses.

oauth.description: Sign in with Google / Microsoft needs your own app registration (a client ID). Create a Desktop app OAuth client in Google Cloud Console, or a Mobile and desktop app in Microsoft Entra with redirect URI http://localhost. Full walkthrough below.
oauth.googleClientSecretHint: Desktop-app clients get one; it is not treated as confidential by Google.
oauth.msClientIdHint: Application (client) ID of the Entra app registration.
oauth.msClientSecretHint: Leave empty for desktop (public client) registrations. Only Web-type registrations use a secret.

guide.title: Step-by-step setup
guide.g1: Create a project: at console.cloud.google.com open the project picker in the top bar, click New project, name it (e.g. Comail), Create, then select it. Then open the menu and go to APIs & Services.
guide.g2: OAuth consent screen: choose External (Internal only if you have a Workspace org), fill in the app name and your email for the support + developer contact, then add your own Gmail address under Test users.
guide.g3: Scopes: click Add or remove scopes, paste https://mail.google.com/ into the manual entry box and add it. That is the only scope Google accepts for IMAP/SMTP XOAUTH2; you do not need to enable the Gmail API.
guide.g4: Credentials: Create credentials, OAuth client ID, application type Desktop app. Desktop clients accept the http://127.0.0.1 loopback automatically, so there is no redirect URI to configure.
guide.g5: Copy the Client ID (ends in .apps.googleusercontent.com) and the Client secret (starts GOCSPX-) into the fields below. They save on blur or Enter.
guide.g6: Once sign-in works, return to the consent screen and click Publish app (Production). While in Testing, Google expires refresh tokens after 7 days and the account flips to needs-reauth weekly; publishing stops that.
guide.m1: At entra.microsoft.com (or portal.azure.com) go to App registrations, New registration. Name it Comail: users see this name on the consent screen.
guide.m2: Supported account types: pick the multitenant option that also includes personal Microsoft accounts (Any org directory + personal). Required: Comail signs in via the shared /common endpoint, and a single-tenant app fails with error AADSTS50194. Fixable later under Authentication.
guide.m3: Redirect URI: choose platform Mobile and desktop applications (not Web), value http://localhost. The Web variant expects a secret and fails with a redirect mismatch; loopback ports are then accepted automatically.
guide.m4: API permissions: Add a permission, Microsoft Graph, then Delegated permissions (not Application; if searching email shows unrelated APIs you are in the Application box). Tick IMAP.AccessAsUser.All, SMTP.Send, offline_access, openid, and email, then Add permissions.
guide.m5: No key or secret to create: skip Certificates & secrets (Comail is a public client). Copy the Application (client) ID from the Overview page into the field below, and leave the Microsoft client secret empty.
guide.m6: Work or school mailboxes only: if sign-in ends with "Need admin approval", a tenant admin must consent once via Grant admin consent on the API permissions page. Personal Outlook.com accounts never need this.
guide.msNote: Personal Outlook.com / Hotmail / Live account? You still register the app here. Sign into the portal with your personal account (it is free, no Azure subscription needed) and pick the multitenant + personal option in step 2. The account you register with and the mailbox you connect can be different.
guide.docsHint: Prefer the full walkthrough with screenshots and troubleshooting?

accounts.themeHint: Theme for this account. "Global default" follows your Preferences theme.
accounts.configureOauthFirst: Fill in the OAuth apps section above first. The step-by-step guide is right there.

snippets.title: Snippets
snippets.expandHint: Type <kbd>;shortcut</kbd> in the composer to expand a snippet.
snippets.empty: No snippets yet. Create one to speed up replies.
snippets.subjectPlaceholder: Fills the subject if it's empty

splits.title: Inbox splits
splits.builtinNote: Important is always first and Other is always last. Reorder custom and auto tabs between them; Other catches anything no earlier tab matched.
splits.empty: No custom splits yet. Create one to give senders their own tab.
splits.priorityHint: Rules are checked top to bottom - the first match wins. Drag the number, or use the arrows, to reorder.
splits.senderLabel: Sender is (use @domain to match a whole domain)
splits.labelHint: Match mail carrying any of these labels, e.g. an INVOICE label an AI automation applied. Takes effect on the next re-sort.
splits.automatedHint: Automated is bulk mail - newsletters, notifications, no-reply senders (detected from List-Unsubscribe / bulk headers). "From a person" is written by a human. "Any" ignores this.
splits.targetHint: Its own tab gives this rule a new inbox tab. Pick Important, Other, or a category to drop matching mail into an existing tab instead.

labels.title: Labels
labels.empty: No labels yet. Create one to tag and filter your mail.

notifications.label: Desktop notifications
notifications.hint: Notify when new mail arrives while the window is unfocused.

sound.label: Sound
sound.hint: Play a sound on new mail and when sending.

dockBadge.label: Dock badge
dockBadge.hint: Show the unread count on the app icon.

dockBadgeSource.label: Badge count
dockBadgeSource.hint: Which unread mail the badge counts.

autoAdvance.label: Auto-advance
autoAdvance.hint: After Done/Snooze in a conversation, open the next thread instead of returning to the list.

groupByDate.label: Group by date
groupByDate.hint: Show Today / Yesterday / … headers between conversations in the list.

autoLabels.label: Auto Labels
autoLabels.hint: Automatically categorize incoming mail as Marketing, News, Social, or Pitch.

about.versionHint: Check for and install the latest release from GitHub.
about.logsHint: Open the folder where log files are stored on this computer.

calendar.hint: Two-way sync: Google and Microsoft need a one-time re-consent; Fastmail/iCloud/self-hosted CalDAV servers use an app password.
calendar.disconnected: Calendar disconnected - events stay on this device

meetingReminder.label: Meeting reminders
meetingReminder.hint: Desktop notification before events start; click it to join the call.
